sourceMovie.title: I Am Number Four
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: I Am Number Four (2011) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. D.J. Caruso's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Amazing Spider-Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I Am Number Four clicked for you, The Amazing Spider-Man 2 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after I Am Number Four?
faq.answer: Try The Amazing Spider-Man 2, Star Trek, Insurgent—each shares DNA with I Am Number Four while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I Am Number Four?
faq.answer: I Am Number Four (2011) is best described as Action, Thriller, Science Fiction, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I Am Number Four worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
